title: Skill store integrity verification at load time, not just at ingest
description: The current security audit (soul_manager.audit_skill()) runs once when a skill is first ingested. After that, the skill is trusted indefinitely. Add a content hash stored at audit time, and verify it each time the skill is loaded into agent context. If the hash doesn't match, re-audit before loading.
rationale: The reading note's core distinction is between single-output tampering (local, affects one task) and search-space contamination (structural, affects all future tasks). Mira's skill store is exactly this kind of search space: skills are loaded as context that shapes planning and reasoning across all subsequent tasks. A skill that passes audit at ingest time but is later modified (by a compromised feed re-fetch, a filesystem write, or a supply chain attack on the soul/ directory) poisons every future task that loads it — without triggering any alert. The reading note's insight maps directly: auditing at ingest only is guarding the door but not the room.
diff_description: In soul_manager.audit_skill(), after a skill passes audit, compute sha256(skill_content) and store it in a sidecar field (e.g., in the skill file's frontmatter as `audit_hash: <hex>` or in a separate `soul/audit_hashes.json`). In the skill-loading path (wherever skills are read into agent context), recompute the hash and compare. On mismatch: log a WARNING, re-run the full audit, and if it fails block the skill; if it passes, update the stored hash. This adds one hash comparison per skill load — negligible cost — and catches post-ingest modification of the skill store, which the current architecture has no defense against.
source_note: 2026-04-07_1-模块化搜索作为技术进步的底层逻辑.md
